chapter_context.summary: Persian archives are searched, and Cyrus's decree is discovered; Darius I decree supports the work and threatens opponents; the Temple is completed and dedicated with great Passover celebrations.

outline.text: Search in Persian Archives; Cyrus's Original Scroll Found at Ecbatana
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Search in Persian Archives; Cyrus's Original Scroll Found at Ecbatana (6:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezra.

outline.text: Darius issues Decree Supporting Rebuilding and Threatening Opponents
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Darius issues Decree Supporting Rebuilding and Threatening Opponents (6:6-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezra.

outline.text: Temple Completed in the Sixth Year of King Darius (515 BC)
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Temple Completed in the Sixth Year of King Darius (515 BC) (6:13-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezra.

outline.text: Joyful Temple Dedication with Abundant Sacrifices and Courses
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Joyful Temple Dedication with Abundant Sacrifices and Courses (6:16-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezra.

outline.text: Joyful Celebration of the Passover and Feast of Unleavened Bread
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Joyful Celebration of the Passover and Feast of Unleavened Bread (6:19-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezra.

key_themes.title: Providential Restoration
key_themes.description: God uses the sovereign decrees of Persian kings (Cyrus, Darius, Artaxerxes) to fulfill His promises of restoration for His people.

key_themes.title: Covenant Sanctity
key_themes.description: The reconstruction of the physical Temple must be matched by the spiritual reformation and moral purity of the community.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 6, how do you see God's hand moving surrounding leaders and circumstances to direct your path?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine areas where compromise has crept into your spiritual life?

cultural_historical.title: The Return from Babylon
cultural_historical.text: The return took place in multiple waves: first under Zerubbabel to rebuild the Temple, and decades later under Ezra to restore covenant instruction.
